publicationStmt.p: Transcribed from Bodleian Library, Lovelace Byron collection, Box 165, folios 116-119. Transcription by Lisa Gold (2022). TEI conversion for Sextant pipeline, 2026.

div.p: Dearest W. After much consideration, I have quite made up my mind as to the course best to be pursued & hope it may appear to you rational & advisable also. I go to Town at 4 this afternoon, not to return, at any rate, but either to set out on

Friday, or Satdy for Ashley from St James' Sqre, in only to delay doing so for the purpose of my vaccination. You & children, (& I think Thyer who would be useful for making packing arrangements &c), to join me in Town as soon as you can on Thursdy.
I think it appears on every account unadvisable for me to return home, as the house must be decidedly
pronounced infected. I have been talking much to Thyer, who I think is on the subject well-judging, & you know has not been at all an alarmist, but very much the reverse.
She thinks it most probable that some others will shortly be taken ill, & that I can be less securely kept from infection here, considering the great number

of persons in the house, & the various indirect modes of communication which cannot be cut off;-- housemaids making my bed &c, &c. I am sure I am doing the wisest & best, & only fear I may appear to you to have judged precipitately.--
I have made very arrangement about clothes, books &c.--
The Ashley box is to be sent off from Town, where everything I do not take with me today is to follow
with you, & per carrier on Thursday, & the division of things to go with us to Ashley, & things to go per waggon, made in Town.--

Since many of the clothes are now dirty, & everything done so much at an hour's notice, this is the best I can do. I find my maid very obliging & clever about packing, & she finds much more room than Hewett did.
I hope you will not feel I am ill-judged,
& you will find I have neglected nothing I believe that was necessary to be thought about.--

I have desired all the soup to be taken off now, as I find that while we are away the leavings are really nothing. I have said that Kayes will name any particular case of illness which may occur, so as to require it. Will you notify the same to him.

I have said that Mary dairy will not go to Ashley. I think you decided the plan did not answer, & for the cost of her journey we may have a Devonshire housemaid I should think, for the time. But of course you will alter my arrangements if you dislike them.-- The lists of plate, china &c in Thyer's keeping, I think should be given up to Kayes, & have said so, unless you desire otherwise.-- The medicine which is to go to
Ashley, as I find we must have a good stock with us there.--
I have directed about stores, coffee, rice for children's puddings, candles, &c &c. You must add tea please.
My harp is all arranged about also, & in short I think I have left very little undone.--
Will you add stationery & what books you want, to the books & music I have put out.-- All the library books, except Wilberforce, I have got with me to be returned, as I have read them.
